steps.title: Calculate the area of circle K
steps.content:
Use the circle area formula with radius 2 mm:
$$A_K = \pi(2)^2 = 4\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add the areas to find the total
steps.content:
Area of circle K is $4\pi$ mm² and area of circle L is $144\pi$ mm² (given). Adding them:
$$4\pi + 144\pi = 148\pi \text{ mm}^2$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
